exact_meaning_duplicate_pairs.why: Both = number of customer visits/guests per period. SBUX 'store_traffic' vs the 6-company 'restaurant_traffic' are identical metrics; 'store' vs 'restaurant' is the same store unit.
Both = average spend per customer/transaction. SBUX coined 'average_ticket'; 3 others coined 'average_check'. Same metric, different word for the same per-customer-spend variable.
Both = total number of units/locations. SBUX 'store_count' = the same footprint count as 'restaurant_count' (6 companies). 'store' vs 'restaurant' is the same unit.
Identical concept: count of restaurant units/locations. 'unit' is just an explicit synonym for the store already counted; same footprint variable.
Identical metric: average annual sales per restaurant. WING merely prefixed 'restaurant_'; the AUV concept is the same.
Both = average sales per restaurant unit (AUV). CMG's 'average_restaurant_sales' is the literal definition of average_unit_volume.
Pure spelling variant (underscore vs none) of the same metric: total sales across company-owned plus franchised units.
Both = menu price increases / pricing actions as a sales-driver. SBUX 'pricing' is the same lever as 'menu_pricing' (3 companies); 'pricing' is just the unprefixed form.
Identical corporate action: company buying back its own stock. 'buyback' and 'repurchase' are exact synonyms.
Both = departure/appointment of senior officers (both grounded in 8-K Item 5.02). Same causal event under two strings.
Identical capital-structure event: issuing/offering convertible senior notes. 'issuance' and 'offering' are the same act here.
Both = restaurant-/store-level operating margin (profit at the unit before corporate overhead). Same metric; SHAK adds 'level'.

Both = inflation in food/commodity input costs. BLMN 'food_cost_inflation' and the commodity-input inflation of TXRH/YUM name the same input-cost-inflation cause (chicken/beef/avocado are commodities = food costs).

near_duplicate_not_merged_notes: ceo_transition vs management_change/executive_transition: ceo_transition is NARROWER (CEO specifically) — kept as related-but-not-exact, candidate for a hierarchy not a SAME_AS.

Brand-scoped comps (chilis_comparable_sales, maggianos_comparable_sales, franchise_comparable_sales, olive_garden_/longhorn_same_store_sales, taco_bell_/kfc_/pizza_hut_/burger_king_/tim_hortons_/popeyes_/firehouse_subs_/international_same_store_sales) are SUBSETS of same_store_sales, not exact dupes of the rolled-up phrase.

Per-brand restaurant counts (burger_king_/tim_hortons_/popeyes_/firehouse_subs_/international_restaurant_count, franchised_restaurant_count, franchise_unit_count, international_licensed_units) are scoped subsets of restaurant_count/restaurant_unit_count, not exact dupes.
food_costs (CMG) vs food_cost_inflation/commodity_cost_inflation: food_costs is the cost LEVEL; the others name the INFLATION (rate of change). Related, borderline — listed as near-dupe, not exact.
cost_inflation (CBRL) is a BROADER bare-category version of food_cost_inflation/labor_cost_inflation/commodity_cost_inflation — flagged as a pass violation (too broad), not a clean exact dupe.
comparable_retail_sales (CBRL) is a distinct segment metric (retail merchandise comps), NOT the same as same_store_sales (restaurant comps) — correctly kept separate.
